title: Pilot Training Split
wikitext:
[]: Pilot Training [Split]             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Dec 02, 2013 9:58 pm Split from Sexualization of Minor Characters . -Reichu Gob Hobblin wrote: Frankly, it's for fanservice. If you wanted to depict a fourteen-year-old girl who's gone through a lifetime of physical training, especially intense military style training, they wouldn't have the proportions you see in Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Dec 02, 2013 10:05 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Yes. Clearly this is exactly the kind of training you need to operate buttons and joysticks in zero gravity.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Dec 02, 2013 10:07 pm The goddamn robot is doing pushups. Come on. Also, the only physically intensive thing we ever see the pilots do, besides bumble around in some air ducts, is the dance training.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Dec 02, 2013 10:55 pm A very good point, and we never really know what Asuka's background is: she talks a big game, but we don't know what that really means. She certainly seems stronger than Shinji, but it's hard to tell if that's pure aggression or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Dec 02, 2013 11:16 pm LMAO at the idea of Asuka being as buff as Mikasa from SNK/AOT underneath her plugsuits for Q.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Dec 03, 2013 12:58 am Bagheera wrote: Yes, quite. Because military pilots don't have to be in good physical condition at all, amirite?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Tue Dec 03, 2013 1:15 am Blech, not for me. I'm happy being a grunt, thank you very much.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Tue Dec 03, 2013 1:18 am Gob Hobblin wrote: Blech, not for me. I'm happy being a grunt, thank you very much.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Tue Dec 03, 2013 1:31 am Thank you kindly! On the above...above post, it's really not as fun as it looks. Imagine spending eight continuous hours, in a chair, staring at CCTV quality footage, in a tiny, sometimes air-conditioned room that smells like butt-sweat (because dozens of other guys have been sweating into the chair before you...so you are sitting in gallons of buttsweat) while people above you give you requests and orders that have absolutely no bearing in how your system operates. Give it a decade to peter down, and it might be a better job. Right now? Not so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Dec 03, 2013 2:02 am Oh, absolutely. Here's a fun article for anyone interested: http://www.cracked.com/article_20725_6-myths-about-drone-warfare-you-probably-believe.html I do wish Anno had gone into more details about the necessity of pilot conditioning. Does being able to bench press more pounds than you did last year make one a better pilot? Does the weight of LCL create a significant amount of resistance? Does a gymnast have an advantage over an obese pilot, or is the "fitness" of your Eva all that matters?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Dec 03, 2013 2:24 am In Gunbuster it's pretty ridiculous. That they DON'T do exactly that sort of thing in eva however is also ridiculous, considering the way the different control systems work. All the children (and I do mean all) should be on a rigorous training schedule both in and out of the evas, including armed and unarmed melee combat, all manner of firearm practice, teambuilding exercises, small unit tactics, combined arms tactics, military history, eva's physical and electronic systems, briefings on the latest angel research, combat ethics, routine psychological counseling and simulation battles against the craziest shit a dedicated war gaming staff can come up with. The idea that they would bother with normal classes most days and occasionally spend hours just meditating in a tube is utterly laughable.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Tue Dec 03, 2013 2:36 am I dunno...spending hours meditating in a tube doesn't seem that different from normal military training. We do see them do some simulation work, so I guess it's implied that's what they're doing off-screen. Still, having an Ender's Game level of intensity, as far as a military regimen would go, would have been hella fascinating. Especially since the Trio aren't exactly prodigies: they're just the best candidates to pilot their respective Evas. So, we'd be seeing what a normal kid would experience when going through the kind of military ringer one would normally see for that kind of position.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Dec 03, 2013 4:53 am @ NemZ : as Gob said, it's implied that the pilot do all these training outside of school, I doubt Shinji learned to use the positron canon, pallet rifles, prog knifes or his minigun in Rebuild by pure instinct. Same thing for the other pilots : Asuka is seen boasting about the specificities of her Evangelion to Shinji in episode 8, showing that she knows her "machine", Rei is seen doing some tuning on an instrument in the earlier episodes(when Shinji see her talking and smiling to Gendo with his Eva's optic zoom) Also, Shinji probably learned how to cut the control from outside sources(seen just after Bardiel) during those lessons. And I doubt that he could had managed to defeat EVA-02 possessed by Kaworu without some prior CQC training with prog-knives. The school time finds its justification in that the pilots mental state directly influence their synchronization with their Eva, and to be on a relatively good mental state they need some form of normalcy and interactions with people of their age(remember : they are teenagers) Finally, for the "meditating in a tube for hours", it's also justified in that it let the staff make adjustments to the core of their Evas to maximize their synchronization, even if it's a drab(we saw the pilots complain about the sheer boredom of it on various occasions) No excuse about the complete lack of routine psychological counseling, although with a guy like Gendo as the boss and the high-ranking staff being generally pretty dysfunctional, it's no wonder they ake any psychologist!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Dec 03, 2013 10:48 am Gob Hobblin wrote: A very good point, and we never really know what Asuka's background is: she talks a big game, but we don't know what that really means. She certainly seems stronger than Shinji, but it's hard to tell if that's pure aggression or not.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Tue Dec 03, 2013 11:05 am Rei IV wrote: LMAO at the idea of Asuka being as buff as Mikasa from SNK/AOT underneath her plugsuits for Q.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Dec 03, 2013 11:22 am @ Chuckman : that's only right for Rei's case : Shinji was enlisted at the last minute because NERV needed a replacement for Rei ASAP before Sachiel attacks, and Asuka's only role was to be the professional Angel-killing machine. Whether they are stable and happy or complete mental wrecks was of no consequences on both Gendo's or SEELE's plans, as long as they are still able to kill Angels. That's also probably why NERV(meaning Gendo and SEELE) never bothered to check on their mental state : they're good to fight, they're good to go, and soon the Dummy System would be completed anyway. And soon after Instrumentality would make all their suffering disappear. Rei was the one whose mental state still needed to be dependent of Gendo, detached from the rest of mankind and eager to return to oblivion(i.e. Lilith's body), and Gendo's inability to see Shinji's influence on Rei is what led to his downfall in EoE. In Rebuild, the situation is slightly different in that Shinji's mental state too had to be closely controlled by Gendo so his son would get close to Rei to the point that he would awake EVA-01 for her. But again Asuka(and Mari)'s mental wellbeing is of no importance, as long as they perform their duty. That makes me wonder if WILLE do regular psychological counseling for their pilots. But anyway, Gendo and SEELE only see the Children as tools, war machines to be used for the destruction of the Angels(with the exception of Rei, and Shinji for Gendo in Rebuild), so for these tools to be efficient, they probably got as much training as possible, while doing lip service for their mental wellbeing, just enough so they continue to pilot and fight(that's what them attending to school is for, and I wouldn't even be surprised if it's Misato who imposed this idea to Gendo!)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 03, 2013 6:48 pm Chuckman wrote: The goddamn robot is doing pushups. Come on.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Dec 03, 2013 7:11 pm Bagheera wrote: And, y'know, scream in pain because their Evas are making them think their limbs are being maimed and torn off every time they sortie.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Dec 04, 2013 12:07 am Dataprime wrote: I got your back, man SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Dec 04, 2013 12:54 am They make it pretty clear that Asuka knows what she's doing with scuba gear, so that counts towards training right? Also SPOILER: Show
summary:
[]: Pilot Training [Split]             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Dec 02, 2013 9:58 pm Split from Sexualization of Minor Characters . -Reichu Gob Hobblin wrote: Frankly, it's for fanservice. If you wanted to depict a fourteen-year-old girl who's gone through a lifetime of physical training, especially intense military style training, they wouldn't have the proportions you see in Evangelion

[]:    &nbs
